{{Quote|Get ''aboard...!'' Rather than ''compete'' with such disgusting nobility... ''I'll'' stay behind!|Simon Greyshade|<ref name="Star Wars (1977) 23">[[Star Wars (1977) 23|''Star Wars'' (1977) 23]]</ref>}}
'''''Star Wars 23: Flight Into Fury''''' is the twenty-third issue in the [[Star Wars (1977)|''Marvel Star Wars'']] series of comics.

==Plot summary==
As [[Simon Greyshade/Legends|Simon Greyshade]] and [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia]] prepare to leave [[The Wheel/Legends|The Wheel]], [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Zertik Strom/Legends|Strom]], realizing that he has been betrayed by Greyshade, sends his [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] to stop them. In the meantime Greyshade, tired of being upstaged by [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker's]] noble actions, decides to allow Luke and Leia to leave with their [[Droid/Legends|droids]] on his ship while he stays behind to cover their escape. After they depart, Strom confronts the wounded Greyshade, intent on carrying out some revenge. Greyshade foils that as well by tossing a [[Proton grenade/Legends|proton grenade]] in Strom's direction, killing him but also fatally wounding himself and critically damaging his droid [[Master-Com]]. In the meantime, [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] is leaving the main event of [[The Big Game]] with his partner [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], who, although seemingly getting killed in the event, now reveals himself to be perfectly healthy. The two make their way to the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] and leave The Wheel just in time to confront [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] battlecruiser. But Luke, while piloting the space yacht, stuns Vader through the use of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] for just long enough for both [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] craft to escape into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]].
